meaning: n.祖鲁兰(南非地名,旧时为祖鲁王国)
examples: He spent his childhood in Natal and Zululand.
In 1900, there were fewer than 20 remaining in the world, all in Zululand and Natal.
It is still there, somewhere out in the bush of what was once Zululand, difficult to find, easy to miss.
In the 1970s, South Africa created KwaZulu, a state for blacks in part of historical Zululand: its capital was Ulundi, where the Zulus were finally defeated.
